converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
John F. Embree papers
Embree, John F. (John Fee), 1908-1950.
Wiswell, Ella Lury.
6475
8.2 cubic feet. 8.2 cubic feet.
1932-2001. Correspondence, manuscripts, census records, publications, clippings, photographs, notes, journals, scrapbooks. Records of a social study undertaken by John Embree and his wife Ella Embree Wiswell of Suye-mura, a Japanese village, from 1935-1936. Collection material in English 4213849
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Date completed:
Apr. 22, 2003
EAD encoding:
J.M. Nicholas, July 2003
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
John Embree received his BA from the University of Hawaii in 1931, his MA from the University of Toronto in 1934 and his PhD from the University of Chicago in 1937. He was Professor of Anthropology at the University of Hawaii, 1937-1941 and from 1943-1945, Associate Professor of Anthropology and head of the Japanese area studies of the Civil Affairs Training School for the Far East which the War Department set up at the University of Chicago for the training of military government officers for Japan and the Occupied Areas. From 1948-1950 he was Associate Professor of Sociology and Research Associate of Anthropology at Yale and in 1950 he was appointed Director of Southeast Asia Studies at Yale.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Correspondence, newspaper clippings, census records, photographs, field notes and journals concerning the Japanese village Suye-mura, which John Embree and his wife Ella Embree Wiswell studied from 1935-36. In 1935 Embree received a grant from the Social Science Research Council to make a social study of a Japanese village. Includes a copy of John Embree's The Chinese Screen and lecture notes on the Civil Affairs Training School for the Far East.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Suye-mura
Boxes 1-5
Series II. Publications Concerning Suye-mura, by Embree and Others
Box 6
Series III. Writings of John F. and Ella Embree
Box 2 Folders 26-32Boxes 3-5
Series IV. Ephemera
Box 5
Series V. Photographs
Volumes 1-8
Cite As:
John F. Embree papers, #6475. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Photographs. Japan -- Study and teaching. Japan -- History. Japan -- Social life and customs. Anthropology -- Study and teaching. Suye-mura (Japan) Social Science Research Council (U.S.) University of Chicago. Civil Affairs Training School

Publications Concerning Suye Mura, by Embree and OthersBy John Fee Embree:Suye Mura: A Japanese Village. Chicago: University of Chicago Press, 1939.6"Acculturation among the Japanese of Kona, Hawaii: Memoirs of the American Anthropological Association," Number 59. 1941.6"The Japanese." Washington, D.C.: Smithsonian Institution War Background Studies, Number 7. 1943.6(Compiled and annotated) Japanese Peasant Songs. Philadelphia: American Folklore Society. 1944.6The Japanese Nation: A Social Survey. New York: Rinehard & Co. 1945.6Suye Mura: A Japanese Village. Chicago: University of Chicago Press. (With a foreward by Richard K. Beardsley.) 7th impression. 1964.6Suye mura: Nihon no mura (Suye Mura: A Japanese Village) Translated by Uemura Notokaku. Tokyo: Nihon keizai hyoran sha. 19786By Arthur F. Raper:The Japanese Village in Transition. Tokyo: General Headquarters, Supreme Commandeer for the Allied Powers, Natural Resources Section, Report #136. 1950. (A survey of 13 villages, one of which is Suye)6By I. Roger Yoshino:"Selected Social Changes in a Japanese Village," 1935-1953. University of Southern California, Ph.D. dissertation. 1954.6By Morimitsu Ushijima:"Suye Mura in Transition." Atlanta University, Master's thesis. 1959.6"Henbo sura suye mura (Suye mura in Transition)." Kyoto: Minerva Shobo. 1971.6Kumamoto: Zusetsu Nihon minzoku shi (Kumamoto prefecture: Illustrated Japanese Folklore). Kumamoto: Iwaski bijitsu sha. 19876Suye Mura, 1935-1985 (Photomonograph). Tokyo: Nihon keizai hyoron sha. 1988.6By Barbara Kawakami:"The Position of Women in Rural Japan: Tradition and Transition in Suye Mura." University of Hawaii, Master's thesis. 19836By Chikara Yamano, Toshitaka Aiko, et al:Ima katari tsutaeru: Suye no kurashi (Speaking of Life in Suye). Privately printed, ND.6By Tadayoshi Tsunematsu:Kumamoto-ken Suye monogatari: furuki-o-tazunete (Kumamoto Prefecture, Suye's Story: Looking Back). Privately printed, 19916By Yoshi Nishi(Editor and publisher): Tsuiso (Memoir). Scope and Contents
8 large albums of 1,720 black and white photographs of Suye Mura taken by John and Ella EmbreePhotographs 1-2161935-19361Photographs 217-4321935-19362Photographs 433-6431935-19363Photographs 649-8641935-19364Photographs 865-10801935-19365Photographs 1081-12961935-19366Photographs 1297-15001935-19367Photographs 1500-17201935-19368 ======= Series I.
